text.title: PHPUnit Testing Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real PHPUnit suites.

antipattern.symptom: Suite green when run sequentially; red when run with --order-by=random.
antipattern.root-cause: Test B depends on Test A's mutation of a shared fixture.
antipattern.fix: Switch to per-test factories; use DatabaseTransactions trait so DB writes rollback per test.

antipattern.symptom: 20 nearly-identical tests differ only in input/expected; maintenance is a chore.
antipattern.fix: Add `public static function casesProvider(): array { return [...]; }` + `#[DataProvider('casesProvider')]` on the test method.

antipattern.symptom: Tests are slow (every test boots Laravel); unit test runtime grows to minutes.

root-cause.source: PHPUnit reference — test doubles; Laravel reference — testing

antipattern.root-cause: All tests use facade fakes (`Mail::fake()`) requiring framework boot.
antipattern.fix: Convert service tests to use constructor-injected mocks (createMock(MailService::class)); reserve facade fakes for feature tests.

antipattern.symptom: Test reads like a maze; reviewer struggles to identify what's being tested.
antipattern.fix: Refactor: collect arrange at top, single act in middle, assertions at bottom with blank lines separating phases.
